Study of Procurement Practices and Project Delivery, , , directed the Comptroller General to conduct a study to assess the impact that a utility company’s failure to relocate its facilities in a timely manner has on the delivery and cost of Federal-aid highway and bridge projects, including an assessment of methods States use to mitigate such delays, and directed the Comptroller General to transmit to Congress a report on the results of the study with any appropriate recommendations not later than 1 year after .Pub. children.heading: .—Cost of relocation
children.label: 1 .—Cost of relocation
children.text: The term “cost of relocation” includes the entire amount paid by a utility properly attributable to the relocation of a utility facility, minus any increase in the value of the new facility and any salvage value derived from the old facility.

children.heading: .—Early utility relocation project
children.label: 2 .—Early utility relocation project
children.text: The term “early utility relocation project” means utility relocation activities identified by the State for performance before completion of the environmental review process for the transportation project.

children.heading: .—Environmental review process
children.label: 3 .—Environmental review process
children.text: The term “environmental review process” has the meaning given the term in section 139(a).

children.heading: .—Transportation project
children.label: 4 .—Transportation project
children.text: The term “transportation project” means a project.

children.heading: .—Utility facility
children.label: 5 .—Utility facility
children.text: The term “utility facility” means any privately, publicly, or cooperatively owned line, facility, or system for producing, transmitting, or distributing communications, power, electricity, light, heat, gas, oil, crude products, water, steam, waste, stormwater not connected with highway drainage, or any other similar commodity, including any fire or police signal system or street lighting system, that directly or indirectly serves the public.

children.heading: .—Utility relocation activity
children.label: 6 .—Utility relocation activity
children.text: The term “utility relocation activity” means an activity necessary for the relocation of a utility facility, including preliminary and final design, surveys, real property acquisition, materials acquisition, and construction.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: If a State pays for the cost of relocation of a utility facility necessitated by the construction of a transportation project, Federal funds may be used to reimburse the State for the cost of relocation in the same proportion as Federal funds are expended on the transportation project.

children.label: A
children.text: violates the law of the State; or

children.label: B
children.text: violates a legal contract between the utility and the State.

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: 3 .—Requirement
children.text: A reimbursement under paragraph (1) shall be made only if the State demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the State paid the cost of the utility relocation activity from funds of the State with respect to transportation projects for which Federal funds are obligated subsequent to , for work, including utility relocation activities.April 16, 1958

children.heading: Reimbursement eligibility for early relocation prior to transportation project environmental review process.—
children.label: 4 Reimbursement eligibility for early relocation prior to transportation project environmental review process.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: In addition to the requirements under paragraphs (1) through (3), a State may carry out, at the expense of the State, an early utility relocation project for a transportation project before completion of the environmental review process for the transportation project.

children.label: i
children.text: the early utility relocation project is necessary to accommodate a transportation project;

children.label: ii
children.text: the State provides adequate documentation to the Secretary of eligible costs incurred by the State for the early utility relocation project;

children.label: I
children.text: would not result in significant adverse environmental impacts; and

children.label: II
children.text: would comply with other applicable Federal environmental requirements;

children.label: I
children.text: the environmental review process for the transportation project;

children.label: II
children.text: the decision relating to the need to construct the transportation project; or

children.label: III
children.text: the selection of the transportation project design or location;

children.label: v
children.text: the early utility relocation project complies with all applicable provisions of law, including regulations issued pursuant to this title;

children.label: vi
children.text: the early utility relocation project follows applicable financial procedures and requirements, including documentation of eligible costs and the requirements under section 109(), but not including requirements applicable to authorization and obligation of Federal funds;l

children.label: vii
children.text: the transportation project for which the early utility relocation project was necessitated was included in the applicable transportation improvement program under section 134 or 135;

children.label: viii
children.text: before the cost incurred by a State is approved for Federal participation, environmental compliance pursuant to the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) has been completed for the transportation project for which the early utility relocation project was necessitated; and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: ix
children.text: the transportation project that necessitated the utility relocation activity is approved for construction.

children.heading: .—Savings provision
children.label: C .—Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph affects other eligibility requirements or authorities for Federal participation in payment of costs incurred for utility relocation activities.
